arts.ttl: Bukayo Saka injury update following nasty challenge on Arsenal star
arts.sum: Bukayo Saka was on the receiving end of a poor tackle during Arsenal’s win over Ipswich Town on Sunday, sparking concerns he could have sustained a fresh injury. The winger made his first start in the Premier League since December following surgery on his hamstring, which kept him sidelined for nearly four months. Saka appears […] The post Bukayo Saka injury update following nasty challenge on Arsenal star first appeared on The Football Faithful.

arts.ttl: ‘I thought’: Kieran McKenna shares what he thinks of Arsenal as a team after their 4-0 win vs Ipswich
arts.sum: Ipswich Town manager Kieran McKenna will have been aware of Arsenal’s quality well before they even arrived at Portman Road. Arsenal had just dispatched Champions...

arts.ttl: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta addresses Bukayo Saka injury concerns ahead of Paris Saint-Germain showdown
arts.sum: Arsenal head coach Mikel Arteta provides a positive update on the fitness of Bukayo Saka following Sunday's Premier League clash with Ipswich Town.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta delivers Bukayo Saka injury update after Arsenal forward was taken off in 4-0 win at Ipswich ahead of PSG semi-final showdown
arts.sum: Saka was withdrawn after Leif Davis' rash challenge caught him on his Achilles in the first half - resulting in him being shown a red card.

arts.ttl: Arsenal's next five fixtures compared to Liverpool after thrashing Ipswich Town
arts.sum: Arsenal will now look ahead to their remaining fixtures, which include just five more in the Premier League as well as a Champions League semi-final against Paris Saint-Germain on the horizon. Mikel Arteta's side closed the gap on leaders Liverpool to 10 points in the Premier League table with the win over Ipswich, before the Merseyside outfit travelled to face Leicester City later on Sunday afternoon.

arts.ttl: Arsenal dealt Bukayo Saka injury scare after horror tackle as Mikel Arteta faces new PSG problem
arts.sum: Mikel Arteta was given the fright of his life when Bukayo Saka went down holding his ankle during Arsenal's clash against Ipswich Town. The 25-year-old defender dived into the back of Saka, planting his studs on the England international's ankle.

arts.ttl: Arsenal player ratings vs Ipswich with Merino masterclass and striker success
arts.sum: Bukayo Saka was lucky to escape unharmed from a dangerous stamp of Leif Davis’ stuns on his Achilles before half-time, a challenge that saw the Ipswich man sent off. Despite the Spaniard spurning a great chance to score in the first half, heading a cross from a corner over the bar when the goal was gaping, he was so strong in every duel, progressed play, recovered and calmed.

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta offers Bukayo Saka injury update after ‘dangerous’ tackle
arts.sum: The winger was on the end of a red-card tackle by Leif Davis in the comfortable Premier League win at Ipswich

arts.ttl: Fulham 1-2 Chelsea: Enzo Maresca plays down importance of dramatic Pedro Neto winner in West London derby
arts.sum: Chelsea head coach Enzo Maresca plays down suggestions that Pedro Neto's stunning late winner against Fulham puts a positive spin on the club's season.

arts.ttl: Arsenal on a different level - McKenna
arts.sum: Ipswich Town manager Kieran McKenna says Arsenal were on a "different level" as they beat his side 4-0 in the Premier League.
